note: Source of truth for the module catalog. A CI drift-linter should verify every module here has a matching reference playbook and that SKILL.md phase pointers stay consistent. 'deterministic' modules run a tool and emit SARIF/JSON; 'judgment' modules require model reasoning over the graph + findings.

modules.notes: Encodes the elected boundaries (layer rules, allowed deps) as executable constraints; a violation is a design_concern against the to-be. Language-gated. Pairs with LSP/SCIP for precise navigation. See core/static-analysis.md.
Distinguishes intentional stub / not-yet-built / gap-toward-to-be from defect. Sets is_intentional_stub. Critical for unfinished codebases.
Cyclomatic complexity, function length. Feeds hotspot heatmap with git churn.
THE core module. Extracts the implied contract at each boundary (DB<->ORM<->API<->frontend) and diffs the shapes. Verifiable. Read the full playbook.
Language-gated (run per language tokei detected). On slop, dead code may be a half-built feature: may promote to a scope question.
SCA + IaC misconfig + license via Trivy; OSV for lockfile vulns.

C1. Treats documentation as a set of CLAIMS about the code and checks each against the graph. A README that documents an endpoint the code does not expose is a contract_mismatch whose as_is side is prose — the same gap = diff(to_be, as_is) engine, applied to the docs layer.

C3. Framework-agnostic entry-point scan (HTTP routes, CLI commands, jobs, handlers) → the Domain→Flow→Step view. Entry points are also the reachability roots fp-check needs, which is why this is not merely a navigation aid.
The vibecode duplication symptom. Feeds consolidate remediation (canonical copy).
Drill-down for one node (or pin): its neighbourhood, inbound/outbound edges, source anchors and any pins attached. The read path a human uses to check the map's claim against the code.

MANDATORY gate for JUDGMENT findings. Systematic false-positive verification before a finding becomes a surfaced pin. AI over-reports; this is non-negotiable. Deterministic static findings (type errors, failed constraints) bypass it — they are facts, not over-reports.

Backbone = the tree-sitter-native structural builder (`scripts/runtime/graph_build.py`): files/symbols/tables as EXTRACTED nodes, imports/calls as EXTRACTED edges, from the same grammars treesitter_extract.py already loads — so it adds no dependency, runs offline, and is deterministic and incremental (signature fingerprints). It emits exactly the NetworkX graph.json shape scripts/runtime/graph.py consumes. OPTIONAL alternatives, neither required: graphify (MIT, 31+ languages, models DB schema, but DEMOTED from primary — the step-0 gating verdict rated its cross-layer edges the INFERRED tier we refuse to ride, ~0 usable semantic edges on a real repo, so only its structural spine was ever used and the native builder produces that without the external dep); gitnexus (PolyForm Noncommercial — no commercial use; code-structure only, no DB schema, does not link separate frontend/backend trees). Cross-layer correspondence is computed by the field-shape engine, never taken from an inferred edge.
The question surface over the graph — 'which parts handle auth?' answered from EXTRACTED edges, not from a semantic guess. Deterministic by construction: it returns what the carrier says, or nothing.

Dependency-ordered walkthroughs: start at an entry point and follow the graph in the order a reader must understand it. The `understand` mode's main deliverable — comprehension as the END, not a step toward remediation.
churn x complexity hotspots; co-change coupling (hidden coupling); bus factor. Mostly a git-log script.

A2. Signature-level fingerprints per symbol, so a re-run reparses only what changed and classifies each change (added / removed / signature-changed / body-only). What makes `resume` and re-audit cheap instead of a full rebuild, and what lets the staleness gate say WHICH part went stale rather than just that the graph did.

D. The layered lens over the graph: colour-coded layer cards (DB / API / client / infra), drill-down per layer, hotspots. A view, holding no state of its own — same rule as the pin map: it projects graph.json + ledger.json and is re-rendered, never hand-edited.
Silent-failure logic, wrong business logic, unhandled states. Model reasoning over graph + to-be. Highest false-positive risk: gate hard via fp-check.
Vibecode signature: auth returning true, empty catch, NotImplemented on prod paths, TODO in live code. Semantic + text pass.
Scans working tree AND full git history (secrets committed then 'removed').
Commoditized. Usually question:null (goes straight to remediation, still plan-gated). Promote to needs_input only on scope ambiguity.
Anti coverage-theater. Language-gated, slow: run only on critical modules. Green tests that survive mutation test nothing.

The highest-value static signal, run per language tokei detects. Type errors are facts (extracted confidence), tied directly to contract/logic pins; they skip the heavy fp-check. Also the in-loop signal during Phase-4 remediation. See core/static-analysis.md.
Visual-first, descriptive. Documents the mess as it is. Never the to-be.
Materializes Question objects from needs_input pins. Runs the compression funnel (cluster -> policy -> exception -> proposed default) with the severity threshold.

C2. Blast radius for a concrete diff: which nodes the touched files reach, and which touched files the graph does not know about ('unmapped'). Feeds Phase-3 sequencing and Phase-5 validation, where the unmapped set is the honest admission that the graph is not total.
